title: MODE() function tries to loads entire data in memory, causing session crash
description:
### What happens?

The MODE() function in DuckDB tries to load the entire dataset into memory, leading to excessive memory usage and session crashes when applied to large datasets.

### To Reproduce

Creating sample data to reproduce the error
```
ATTACH 'duckdb/db';
use db;
SET temp_directory = 'duckdb/temp_table';

CREATE TABLE db.sample_data AS 
WITH numbers AS (FROM range(1, 100_000_000) t(id))
SELECT 
    id,
    (RANDOM() % 3) AS download,   -- Generates 0, 1, or 2 for download usage
    (RANDOM() % 3) AS upload,     -- Generates 0, 1, or 2 for upload usage
    'Application_' || (RANDOM() % 1000) AS app,  -- Generates 1000 unique apps
    LEFT(md5(random()::text), 44) AS key  -- 44-byte unique string with cardinality 2000
FROM numbers;
— It creates 3.9Gb data in persistent disk 
```

now let's create duckdb cli session again with below settings 
```
.timer on
SET memory_limit = '1GB';
SET max_memory = '1GB';
SET enable_progress_bar = true;
SET enable_fsst_vectors=true;
SET preserve_insertion_order = false;

ATTACH 'duckdb/db';
use db;
SET temp_directory = 'duckdb/temp_table';

select app, key, mode(download), mode(upload) from db.sample_data group by app, key;
 
```
It will be killed right away 

I've found WORKAROUND for this behavior 
```
WITH download_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        app, 
        key, 
        SUM(CASE WHEN download = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS low_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN download = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS average_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN download = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_count
    FROM db.sample_data
    GROUP BY app, key
),
upload_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        app, 
        key, 
        SUM(CASE WHEN upload = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS low_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN upload = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS average_count,
        SUM(CASE WHEN upload = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_count
    FROM db.sample_data
    GROUP BY app, key
)

SELECT 
    d.app, 
    d.key,
    CASE 
        WHEN d.low_count = GREATEST(d.low_count, d.average_count, d.high_count) THEN 0
        WHEN d.average_count = GREATEST(d.low_count, d.average_count, d.high_count) THEN 1
        ELSE 2 
    END AS mode_download,
    CASE 
        WHEN u.low_count = GREATEST(u.low_count, u.average_count, u.high_count) THEN 0
        WHEN u.average_count = GREATEST(u.low_count, u.average_count, u.high_count) THEN 1
        ELSE 2 
    END AS mode_upload
FROM download_counts d
JOIN upload_counts u 
ON d.app = u.app AND d.key = u.key;

```

But it worked because i had categorical column and i knew all possible distinct values. 
Can you fix this issue in general.
 

### OS:

linux

### DuckDB Version:

v1.2.0

### DuckDB Client:

Cli

### Hardware:

I have 24cpu and 48 GB ram and used it on 55GB dataset but it failed

### Full Name:

Suraj Pattewar

### Affiliation:

Student

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Not applicable - the reproduction does not require a data set

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE db.test_data (id INTEGER, download INTEGER, upload INTEGER, app VARCHAR(255), key VARCHAR(44));

INSERT INTO db.test_data SELECT id, (RANDOM() % 3) AS download, (RANDOM() % 3) AS upload, 'App_' || (RANDOM() % 100) AS app, LEFT(md5(random()::text), 44) AS key FROM (SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER () AS id FROM system_range(1, 100000));

SELECT app, key, COUNT(*) AS count_download, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 0) AS low_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 1) AS average_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 2) AS high_count FROM db.test_data GROUP BY app, key;

WITH download_summary AS (SELECT app, key, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 0) AS low_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 1) AS average_count, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE download = 2) AS high_count FROM db.test_data GROUP BY app, key) SELECT app, key, CASE WHEN low_count = GREATEST(low_count, average_count, high_count) THEN 0 WHEN average_count = GREATEST(low_count, average_count, high_count) THEN 1 ELSE 2 END AS mode_download FROM download_summary;
SELECT app, key, COALESCE(NULLIF(MAX(download), 0), 1) AS max_download FROM db.test_data GROUP BY app, key HAVING COUNT(*) > 10;
key_bug_features: MODE() function loads entire dataset into memory
